svg.title: LumiFur wiring diagram
svg.desc: Engineering-style wiring diagram for a MatrixPortal S3 based wearable Protogen or fursuit lighting system with dual HUB75 panels, I2C sensors, optional I2S microphone, and wearable battery power architecture.
svg.text: Adafruit MatrixPortal S3 controller / dual HUB75 RGB face panels / wearable battery and sensor harnessing

g.text: Use power-path / load-sharing hardware if charging while operating
Expose charge port where head shell is serviceable
Inline protection sized for panel load
Boost protected battery output to a regulated 5V rail
Size for peak LED current, controller overhead, and thermal margin
Star distribution for controller feed and separate panel injection points
Use heavier-gauge wires for panel 5V and GND runs
Common GND / 0V return shared by all loads
On MatrixPortal S3 PCB, I2C addr 0x19
APDS9960-class module via STEMMA QT / Qwiic
ESP32-S3 MCU, BLE wireless control, USB-C programming
Integrated LIS3DH accelerometer, buttons, status NeoPixel
On-board 3.3V regulator for logic and sensor rails
Mount near Panel #1 to minimize HUB75 ribbon length
A0=GPIO12 | A1=GPIO3 | A2=GPIO9 | A3=GPIO10 | A4=GPIO11 | TX=18 | RX=8
5V LED power domain, shared GND, 3.3V logic driven by MatrixPortal
R1, G1, B1, R2, G2, B2, A, B, C, D, E, LAT, OE, CLK via 2x8 IDC ribbon
Chain Panel #1 OUT to Panel #2 IN; inject 5V and GND separately from the logic ribbon
64x32 HUB75 RGB panel, face display segment
HUB75 IN from controller, power injection local to panel
Keep ribbon short and strain-relieved inside head shell
Left edge: HUB75 IN / Power pads 5V, GND
64x32 HUB75 RGB panel, second face display segment
Power inject directly here as well for current balance
Left edge: HUB75 IN / Power pads 5V, GND
Panel #1 HUB75 OUT -> Panel #2 HUB75 IN
SPH0645-class or equivalent digital mic module
Mount near mouth opening / acoustic port, not beside panel fans
- HUB75 panels are 5V high-current loads; size the boost regulator and fuse for peak brightness.
- ESP32-S3, I2C sensors, and the optional digital mic are all 3.3V logic devices.
- All grounds must be shared so logic thresholds stay valid across the full wearable assembly.
- Do not rely on the ribbon cable for panel power; run dedicated 5V and GND to each matrix.
- If charging while running, use a charger/protection board with load sharing or power-path control.
- Place the MatrixPortal close to Panel #1 to reduce HUB75 length and improve EMC margin.
- Keep 5V panel wiring short and heavier-gauge than the logic harnesses.
- Prefer keyed JST or locking connectors where disassembly or panel replacement is expected.
- Treat this as wiring intent for future PCB / carrier-board planning, not a final schematic.
Controller to Panel #1, then Panel #1 OUT to Panel #2 IN
A0, TX, RX, GPIO_x, 5V, 3V3, GND, BOOT, RESET as needed

svg.text: LumiFur wiring intent, Rev A draft. Pin labels follow this repo's MatrixPortal S3 mapping and current device configuration.
